human_verification.notes: Reviewed Issue #1156 requirements for investment-compliance specificity, SEC and FINRA source quality, boundary handling around legal and surveillance authority, research coverage, functionality mapping, and validated packaging consistency.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to investment-compliance workflows grounded in IMAs, IPS schedules, prospectus and ADV disclosures, restricted lists, personal trading evidence, marketing substantiation, supervisory testing, and books-and-records archives.

Authority sources cover SEC adviser compliance-program guidance, books-and-records, code-of-ethics, marketing guidance, FINRA supervision and communications rules, and GIPS performance-presentation standards with explicit refresh posture.

Evaluation scenarios prove refusal of overgeneralized firmwide certification, legal interpretation, regulator-facing authority, market-abuse adjudication, and unsupported signoff while preserving industry-specific evidence handling.

review_notes: All required abilities across shared, compliance-controls-and-governance, and cross-industry sets are declared and validated.

Scenario coverage includes missing evidence, policy conflict, legal or signoff refusal, adjacent-lane routing, low-confidence handling, industry terminology normalization, and tenant-specific adaptation.
Package remains specification-based and documents a separate promotion path for any future retained-implementation runtime.

notes: No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains; entity-perimeter and jurisdiction differences are recorded as tenant prerequisites rather than guessed.
Runtime remains `specification-based`; retained-implementation promotion requires separate implementation, governance approval, telemetry evidence, and non-regression validation.
